ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANTS AMERICAN PRESIDENT LINES AND CROWLEY MARITIME CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS OVERRULED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO ANSWER. THE COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY STATES A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE UNDER CALIFORNIA LAW AND IS NOT UNCERTAIN. PLAINTIFF'S STATE LAW NEGLIGENCE CLAIM IS NOT PREEMPTED BY FEDERAL MARITIME LAW BECAUSE, AS PLED, THE ALLEGED EXPOSURE AND DEFENDANTS' ALLEGED ACTIVITIES DO NOT HAVE A 'MARITIME CONNECTION.' (JEROME B. GRUBART. INC. V. GREAT LAKES DREDGE & DOCK CO. (1995) 513 U.S. 527; SISSON V. RUBY. (1990) 497 U.S. 358; FOREMOST INS. CO. V. RICHARDSON, (1982) 457 U.S. 668; EXECUTIVE JET AVIATION, INC. V. CLEVELAND (1972) 409 U.S. 249. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: JAMES J. MCBRIDE, REPORTER: DIANA PADILLA CSR # 9975
